report_name: UK's HMRC Taps Quantexa AI to Dismantle Cyber-Enabled Tax Fraud Rings

identities.name: HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC)
identities.description: The UK's primary tax authority, which has contracted AI firm Quantexa to use its 'decision intelligence' platform to combat sophisticated tax fraud and error.

identities.name: Quantexa
identities.description: A UK-based AI firm providing its 'decision intelligence' platform to HMRC under a ten-year, £175 million contract to help detect financial crime.

identities.name: Organized crime groups
identities.description: Criminal networks engaged in sophisticated tax fraud, which are the primary target of the new AI-driven detection system being implemented by HMRC.

identities.name: UK taxpayers
identities.description: Individuals and businesses in the UK who will be affected by the new era of automated tax compliance enforcement by HMRC.
